skill.name: youtube-transcript-extractor-api-skill
skill.description: Extract YouTube video transcripts and metadata automatically using the BrowserAct API. This skill retrieves full subtitles, video titles, publisher information, and engagement metrics from any public YouTube video URL.
skill.summary: Automatically extract YouTube video transcripts and metadata using the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The skill reads a BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables and sends it to the BrowserAct API (api.browseract.com) for YouTube transcript extraction. All 14 external_commands findings in SKILL.md are false positives caused by Markdown code block backticks being misinterpreted as shell command execution. The 4 weak cryptographic algorithm findings and system reconnaissance findings are also false positives (documentation text, not code). The env_access and network risk factors are confirmed but represent legitimate, transparent functionality inherent to the skill's purpose. No obfuscation, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was detected.

medium_findings.title: API Credential Accessed from Environment Variables
medium_findings.description: The skill reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables using os.getenv() and transmits it in HTTP request headers to api.browseract.com. This is legitimate functionality but users should be aware that their API credentials are accessed and sent over the network.

medium_findings.title: External API Network Requests
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to the BrowserAct API (api.browseract.com) to start transcript extraction tasks, poll for completion, and retrieve results. Network access is required for the skill to function but represents data leaving the local environment.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API Base URL in Source Code
low_findings.description: The BrowserAct API base URL is hardcoded as a constant. This is standard practice for API client code but means the endpoint is fixed and cannot be changed without modifying the script.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Credential Transmitted Over Network
dangerous_patterns.description: The BROWSERACT_API_KEY is read from environment variables and sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of HTTP requests to an external API. The pattern of reading secrets from env and transmitting them over the network is a security-relevant pattern, though it is the intended and transparent operation of this skill.

content.user_title: Extract YouTube Video Transcripts and Metadata
content.value_statement: YouTube videos contain valuable information but watching them takes significant time. This skill extracts full transcripts and metadata from any public YouTube video URL automatically using the BrowserAct API, giving you the content without the video.
actual_capabilities: Extract full text transcripts from any public YouTube video URL
Retrieve video metadata including title, publisher name, and channel information
Obtain video like counts and channel link URLs
Handle transcript extraction without CAPTCHA challenges or IP restrictions
Provide real-time status updates during the extraction process
Poll for task completion and retrieve results automatically
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key configured as BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Cannot extract transcripts from age-restricted, private, or deleted videos
Processing time may take several minutes depending on video length and server load
Limited to single video URL input per request, not playlist or channel extraction

use_cases.title: Analyze Video Content at Scale
use_cases.description: Extract transcripts from multiple YouTube videos for content analysis, research, or competitive intelligence without watching each video end to end.

use_cases.title: Build Transcript Datasets
use_cases.description: Collect structured transcript data with metadata for training AI models, building knowledge bases, or creating searchable video archives.

use_cases.title: Summarize Video Content
use_cases.description: Extract transcripts and use them to generate summaries, key points, or topic analysis of YouTube videos for quick review.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Transcript Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the transcript from this YouTube video: [URL]
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with simple single-video transcription

prompt_templates.title: Extract with Full Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the full transcript, video title, publisher name, and like count for this YouTube video: [URL]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need both video content and metadata together

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Video Without Watching
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the transcript from [URL] and then summarize the key points and main topics from the content
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to understand video content without watching it

prompt_templates.title: Categorize and Analyze Video Topics
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the transcript from [URL], identify the main topics discussed, organize them into categories, and provide a structured analysis of the content
prompt_templates.scenario: When conducting structured media research across multiple videos

output_examples.output: The video titled 'Introduction to Machine Learning' by TechEducation channel has been processed successfully. Publisher: TechEducation. Channel Link: https://youtube.com/@TechEducation. Transcript begins: 'Welcome to this introduction to machine learning. In this video, we will cover the fundamental concepts of supervised and unsupervised learning...'

output_examples.input: Get metadata and transcript for https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=abc123xyz
output_examples.output: Video Title: Understanding Neural Networks | Publisher: AI Explained | Channel Link: https://youtube.com/@AIExplained | Likes: 1,234 | Transcript length: 2,847 words. Transcript: 'Neural networks are computing systems inspired by biological neural networks in the brain. These systems learn to perform tasks by considering examples...'

output_examples.input: Extract and summarize this YouTube video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xyz789example
output_examples.output: Transcript extracted from 'Web Development in 2024' by CodeAcademy. The video covers three main topics: (1) modern JavaScript frameworks like React and Vue, (2) backend development with Node.js and Python, and (3) deployment strategies using cloud services. The presenter emphasizes best practices for building scalable web applications.

best_practices: Ensure the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before running the extraction script to avoid authorization errors
Monitor the status output during extraction to confirm the task is progressing normally - status updates indicate active processing
Use specific YouTube video URLs rather than search queries, playlist links, or channel URLs for reliable extraction
anti_patterns: Do not retry failed tasks more than once - if the second attempt fails, report the specific error message to the user instead
Do not assume the process has stalled just because it takes time - the script provides timestamped status updates to confirm normal operation
Do not attempt to extract transcripts from non-YouTube video URLs or short-form content that may not have available subtitles

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key set as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. Get your key from the BrowserAct console at https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations.

faq.question: How long does transcript extraction take?
faq.answer: Processing typically takes 1 to 5 minutes depending on video length and server load. The script outputs timestamped status updates so you can monitor progress.

faq.question: Can this extract transcripts from any YouTube video?
faq.answer: It works on most public YouTube videos. Age-restricted, private, deleted, or live-stream-only videos may fail to return transcripts.

faq.question: What metadata does the skill retrieve?
faq.answer: The skill retrieves video title, publisher name, channel link, video URL, and like count in addition to the full transcript.

faq.question: Do I need to handle CAPTCHA or IP blocks?
faq.answer: No. The BrowserAct API handles CAPTCHA challenges, IP restrictions, and geofencing automatically on the server side.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The skill will report an Invalid authorization error and stop. Check your key at the BrowserAct console and make sure the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set correctly.
